info.name: Car Workshop
info.description:
# Car Workshop Management System API

## Overview

This is a RESTful API for managing automotive workshop operations, including customer management, vehicle registration, work orders, service assignments, and invoicing.

## Base URL

`{{baseUrl}}` (typically `http://127.0.0.1:9000/api/v1`)

## Authentication

Most endpoints require **Bearer JWT authentication**. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

 ```

**How to get a token:**

1. Call `POST /auth/login` with email and password
    
2. Copy the `access_token` from the response
    
3. Set it in Postman environment variable: `token`
    
4. Use `{{token}}` in the Authorization header
    

## Response Format: JSON:API

All responses follow the **JSON:API v1.0** specification:

### Successful Response Structure

``` json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "<uuid>",
    "type": "resource-type",
    "attributes": { ... },
    "relationships": { ... },
    "meta": { ... }
  },
  "included": [ ... ],  // Related resources (optional)
  "links": { ... },     // Pagination links (optional)
  "meta": { ... }       // Pagination metadata (optional)
}

 ```

### Error Response Structure

``` json
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "status": "422",
      "title": "Validation Error",
      "detail": "The email field is required.",
      "source": {
        "pointer": "/data/attributes/email"
      },
      "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

## Rate Limiting

- **Limit:** 100 requests per window
    
- **Headers:** `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`
    
- On rate limit exceeded: HTTP 429
    

## Common Status Codes

| Code | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| 200 | Success |
| 201 | Created |
| 204 | No Content (successful deletion) |
| 400 | Bad Request (invalid query parameters) |
| 401 | Unauthorized (missing/invalid token) |
| 403 | Forbidden (insufficient permissions) |
| 404 | Not Found |
| 422 | Validation Error |
| 429 | Too Many Requests |
| 500 | Internal Server Error |

## Query Parameters

All list endpoints support:

- **Filtering:** `?filter[field]=value`
    
- **Sorting:** `?sort=field` (prefix with `-` for descending: `-created_at`)
    
- **Pagination:** `?page=1&per_page=15`
    
- **Including relations:** `?include=relation1,relation2.nested`
    

## Work Order State Machine

```
draft → diagnosed → approved → in_progress → completed → invoiced/complained → closed
              ↓                                    ↓
           canceled ←------------------------------→

 ```

**State Transitions:**

- **draft → diagnosed:** Mechanic submits diagnosis and proposed services
    
- **diagnosed → approved:** Customer approves the diagnosis
    
- **approved → in_progress:** Mechanic starts working
    
- **in_progress → completed:** All services completed
    
- **completed → invoiced:** Admin creates invoice
    
- **completed → complained:** Customer files complaint
    
- **invoiced → closed:** Payment received and order closed
    
- **any → canceled:** Order canceled (cannot cancel if in_progress or completed)

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description:
This endpoint is used to verify a user's email address.

**WARNING: DO NOT TEST VIA SCRAMBLE FORM!**  
This endpoint requires Laravel's **Signed URL**. If you attempt to send  
an API request directly from this documentation page, the system will  
always return a `403 Invalid Signature` error.

**How to test properly:**

1. Call the `Register` or `Resend Verification Email` endpoint.
    
2. Copy the **complete URL** from the received email (it must include the `?expires=...&signature=...` parameters).
    
3. Paste the complete URL into a new browser tab or Postman.
Resend the email verification link to the authenticated user.

item.description:
## Authentication

User authentication and account management endpoints.

**Endpoints:**
- `POST /auth/login`: Login and get JWT token
- `POST /auth/register`: Register new user
- `POST /auth/logout`: Logout (revoke token)
- `POST /auth/refresh`: Refresh JWT token
- `GET /auth/verify-email/:id/:hash`: Verify email address (requires signed URL)
- `POST /auth/email/verification-notification`: Resend verification email
- `POST /auth/forgot-password`: Request password reset link
- `POST /auth/reset-password`: Reset password with token

item.name: health

request.description: Full check if the application is running.

response.name: Success
Unauthenticated

request.description: Check if the application is running. No authentication required.

response.name: Success

item.description:
## Health Check

Application health monitoring endpoints.

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /health/basic`: Basic health check (no auth required)
- `GET /health/full`: Full health check including services (auth required)

item.description:
## User Profile

Manage the authenticated user's own profile.

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /profile`: Get current user's profile
- `PUT /profile`: Update profile information
- `PATCH /profile/avatar`: Upload avatar image
- `POST /profile/change-password`: Change password

## System & Access

Endpoints for authentication, health checks, and user profile management.

**Sub-folders:**
- `auth`: User authentication (login, register, password reset, token management)
- `health`: Application health monitoring
- `profile`: Authenticated user's profile management

response.name: Unauthenticated

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Validation error

item.description:
## Cars

Manage customer vehicles registered in the workshop.

**Attributes:**
- `plate_number`: License plate (unique)
- `brand`: Car manufacturer (e.g., Toyota, Honda)
- `model`: Car model (e.g., Avanza, Civic)
- `year`: Manufacturing year
- `color`: Vehicle color
- `owner_id`: UUID of the customer (user)

**Relationships:**
- `owner`: The user who owns this car
- `workOrders`: Work orders associated with this car

**Query Filters:**
- `filter[plate_number]`: Partial match on plate number
- `filter[brand]`: Partial match on brand
- `filter[owner_id]`: Exact owner UUID
- `sort`: `brand`, `year`, `created_at`
- `include`: `owner`, `workOrders`

item.description:
## Services

Manage the service catalog (labor services, spare parts, consumables).

**Attributes:**
- `name`: Service/part name
- `description`: Detailed description
- `base_price`: Base price (in local currency)
- `is_active`: Whether currently available (toggle if out of stock)

**Relationships:**
- `workOrderServices`: Line items in work orders that use this service

**Query Filters:**
- `filter[is_active]`: Filter by active status
- `filter[name]`: Partial match on name
- `sort`: `name`, `base_price`, `created_at`
- `include`: `workOrderServices`, `complaintServices`

item.description:
## Users

Manage all users in the system (customers, mechanics, admins).

**Attributes:**
- `name`: Full name
- `email`: Email address (unique)
- `avatar_url`: Profile picture URL
- `is_active`: Account status (active/deactivated)
- `email_verified_at`: Email verification timestamp

**Relationships:**
- `roles`: User roles (customer, mechanic, admin, super_admin)

**Query Filters:**
- `filter[is_active]`: Filter by active status
- `filter[name]`: Partial match on name
- `filter[email]`: Partial match on email
- `filter[role]`: Filter by role name
- `sort`: `name`, `email`, `created_at`, `is_active`
- `include`: `roles`

**Special Endpoints:**
- `GET /users/trashed`: List soft-deleted users
- `POST /users/:id/restore`: Restore a deleted user
- `PATCH /users/:id/role`: Change user role
- `PATCH /users/:id/toggle-active`: Toggle active status

## Master Data

Core data management for the workshop system.

**Resources:**
- `cars`: Customer vehicle registry
- `services`: Service catalog (labor, parts, consumables)
- `users`: User management (customers, mechanics, admins)

item.description:
## Work Orders

Manage work orders through their complete lifecycle.

**Attributes:**

- `order_number`: Human-readable order ID (e.g., WO-20260415-2STHZ)
    
- `status`: Current state (draft, diagnosed, approved, in_progress, completed, invoiced, complained, canceled, closed)
    
- `diagnosis_notes`: Mechanic's diagnosis text
    
- `estimated_completion`: Expected completion date
    

**Relationships:**

- `car`: The vehicle being serviced
    
- `creator`: User who created the work order
    
- `workOrderServices`: Service line items
    
- `invoice`: Associated invoice (when invoiced)
    

**State Transitions:**

- `PATCH /:id/diagnose`: draft → diagnosed
    
- `PATCH /:id/approve`: diagnosed → approved
    
- `PATCH /:id/cancel`: any → canceled (except in_progress/completed)
    
- `PATCH /:id/complete`: in_progress → completed
    
- `PATCH /:id/record-complaint`: completed → complained
    
- `PATCH /:id/mark-invoiced`: completed → invoiced
    

**Query Filters:**

- `filter[status]`: Filter by work order status
    
- `filter[car_id]`: Filter by car UUID
    
- `filter[order_number]`: Partial match on order number
    
- `sort`: `order_number`, `created_at`, `status`
    
- `include`: `car`, `car.owner`, `creator`, `workOrderServices`, `workOrderServices.service`

item.description:
## Complaints

Manage customer complaints filed against completed work orders.

Complaints are created via `PATCH /work-orders/:id/record-complaint` (under Work Orders). This folder contains endpoints for **managing** those complaints: viewing, resolving, rejecting, reassigning, and assigning mechanics to complaint service items.

**Complaint Lifecycle:**

```
open → assigned → in_progress → resolved
                                   ↓
                                rejected

 ```

**Complaint Attributes:**

- `description`: Customer's complaint description
    
- `status`: `open`, `assigned`, `in_progress`, `resolved`, `rejected`
    
- `resolution_notes`: Notes from the mechanic/admin when resolving
    
- `resolved_at`: Timestamp when resolved
    

**Relationships:**

- `workOrder`: The parent work order
    
- `complaintServices`: Service items that need re-work
    
- `complaintServiceAssignments`: Mechanic assignments for re-work
    

**Query Filters:**

- `filter[status]`: Filter by complaint status
    
- `filter[work_order_id]`: Filter by work order UUID
    
- `sort`: `created_at`, `status`
    
- `include`: `workOrder`, `complaintServices`, `complaintServices.service`, `complaintServiceAssignments`, `complaintServiceAssignments.mechanic`

item.description:
## Invoices

Manage invoicing for completed work orders.

Invoices are generated when a work order is marked as invoiced via `PATCH /work-orders/:id/mark-invoiced`. This folder contains endpoints for **viewing, sending, paying, and canceling** invoices.

**Invoice Lifecycle:**

```
draft → unpaid → sent → paid
  ↓        ↓       ↓
canceled canceled canceled     

 ```

**Invoice Attributes:**

- `invoice_number`: Unique invoice identifier (e.g., `INV-202604-0001`)
    
- `subtotal`: Sum of all service line items before discount/tax
    
- `discount`: Applied discount amount
    
- `tax`: Calculated tax amount
    
- `total`: Final amount (`subtotal - discount + tax`)
    
- `status`: `unpaid`, `sent`, `paid`, `canceled`
    
- `due_date`: Payment due date (ISO 8601 date)
    

**Relationships:**

- `workOrder`: The associated work order
    
- `workOrder.workOrderServices`: Service line items
    

**Query Filters:**

- `filter[status]`: Filter by invoice status
    
- `filter[work_order_id]`: Filter by work order UUID
    
- `sort`: `invoice_number`, `total`, `due_date`, `created_at`
    
- `include`: `workOrder`

## Workshop Operations

Core workflow management for daily workshop operations.

**Resources:**

- `work-orders`: Work order lifecycle management
    
- `work-order-services`: Individual service line items within work orders
    
- `mechanic-assignments`: Mechanic assignments to service items
    

## Work Order Workflow

```
1. Draft: Customer brings car, admin creates work order
2. Diagnosed: Admin inspects car, proposes services
3. Approved: Customer approves the diagnosis
4. In Progress: Mechanic works on the car
5. Completed: All services finished
6. Invoiced: Invoice generated (or Complaint: customer complains)
7. Closed: Payment received, order closed

 ```
